paradigm_note: All exact public API shapes (action variants, error variants, wire structs, boot call shape) are pinned in DESIGN (feature-delta + ADR-0063/0067) — the crafter implements to that contract; ACs check observable boundary behaviour, so a behaviour-preserving shape tweak does not fail the roadmap. Project-wide discipline (Tier-1 DST + PBT default, Lima execution, EDD capture, mutation gate >=80%, single-cut greenfield, 'never invent API surface') is governed by .claude/rules/{testing,development}.md + CLAUDE.md and is NOT restated per step.

phases.name: Rotation — near-expiry SVID reissue as a reconciler action (#40)
phases.description: Flip the SvidLifecycle near-expiry branch from the inert gated-workflow seam to a direct unconditional rotate IssueSvid action, and single-cut retire the rotation gate. Pure overdrive-core Tier-1 DST; no EDD (in-process reconciler logic stays in the test tiers). Implements D-OC-1/2/3/8. Does NOT depend on Phase 02/03.

steps.name: Flip SvidLifecycle near-expiry branch to unconditional rotate Action::IssueSvid
steps.description: The near-expiry held-SVID branch in SvidLifecycle::reconcile emits a single rotate IssueSvid (reusing the existing action per DESIGN), unconditionally, instead of the inert gated StartWorkflow seam. The near-expiry threshold is half the workload SVID TTL, derived from the policy const (not a bare literal). GREENs the 5 DST rotation scaffolds S-OC-01..05.
criteria: S-OC-01: a Running held alloc with not_after strictly within half-TTL of now emits exactly one rotate-correlation IssueSvid carrying the held spiffe_id and the running node_id, and no StartWorkflow (proptest invariant near_expiry_held_alloc_emits_one_rotate_issue_svid over arbitrary now + arbitrary in-window slack).

S-OC-02: a Running held alloc with not_after strictly beyond now + half-TTL emits no IssueSvid (converged action vector is exactly [Noop]) — proptest invariant held_alloc_outside_near_expiry_window_emits_no_issue_svid over arbitrary now + far-future not_after.

S-OC-04: the rotation threshold tracks half of the workload SVID TTL via the emitted action only — a hardcoded threshold that ignores a TTL policy change flips the emit and reds this (rotation_threshold_tracks_half_of_workload_svid_ttl_via_emitted_action); asserts the emitted action list, never a private threshold constant.

S-OC-05: a held near-expiry alloc emits a rotate-correlation IssueSvid while a separate unheld-but-ever_issued alloc emits an issue-correlation IssueSvid; neither emits StartWorkflow and the two correlations are distinct (proptest invariant rotate_is_distinct_from_restart_recovery_reissue).
All pre-existing SvidLifecycle branch tests (issue / drop / backoff / restart-recovery) stay GREEN, unchanged.
No Fixture Theater: the production branch flip is what flips the scaffolds RED -> GREEN.
review_checklist: git diff confirms svid_lifecycle.rs is modified (branch flip drives GREEN, not test fixtures).
Threshold const derived from the workload-SVID-TTL policy const, not a bare literal.
Action::IssueSvid reused unchanged; node_id from running.node_id (no HeldSvidFacts change).
steps.implementation_notes: Tier-1 DST per testing.md. S-OC-01/02/05 are @property proptests; S-OC-04 uses TTL-derived boundary examples. Assert only on the Vec<Action> + next View, never private fields. Scaffolds exist (b8d0546f) — replace #[should_panic] bodies, do not re-author; confirm fail-for-right-reason first.

steps.name: Single-cut retire the rotation gate + un-skip the near_expiry mutation boundary
steps.description: Single-cut retire the now-dead rotation gate (the gate existed only to suppress an unregistered cert_rotation workflow that no longer exists after the action reframe) and remove the near_expiry mutation suppression, landing the inclusive-<= boundary kill-test (S-OC-03) so the now-live boundary has a mutation killer. All deletions and the kill-test land in one commit.
criteria: S-OC-03: a Running held alloc whose not_after equals exactly now + half-TTL emits one rotate IssueSvid (the <= boundary is inclusive); one at half-TTL + 1s emits none. The two pinned boundary examples are the live mutation kill-test killing both the <=->< and <=->== mutants (near_expiry_boundary_is_inclusive_at_half_ttl).
The crate compiles with no dead_code / unused_imports warnings after the gate retirement.
The remaining SvidLifecycle reconcile tests stay GREEN after the gated-seam test is removed.
The live backoff comparison in reconcile remains a tested mutation target and is unaffected by the near_expiry suppression removal.
Mutation gate: diff-scoped run over svid_lifecycle.rs reports kill-rate >= 80% with the near_expiry <= boundary mutants CAUGHT (no longer skipped).
review_checklist: ROTATION_ENABLED / CERT_ROTATION_WORKFLOW consts + the StartWorkflow / WorkflowName imports removed from svid_lifecycle.rs.
The gated-seam test near_expiry_rotation_seam_is_emit_gated_until_cert_rotation_registered is deleted (delete code AND its test).
.cargo/mutants.toml no longer carries the near_expiry exclude_re entry or its comment block.
A dead_code warning after deletion signals MORE deletion, not an #[allow].
steps.implementation_notes: Tier-1 DST per testing.md; mutation gate >=80% is a hard done-definition. S-OC-03 is the inclusive-<= boundary kill-test (hand-picked examples — generation dilutes the boundary signal); assert only on the emitted IssueSvid count. Suppression removal and kill-test land together so the boundary is never live-but-unkilled. Scaffold exists — replace its body, do not re-author.

phases.name: Boot-side #215 — persistent CA wired into `overdrive serve`
phases.description: overdrive serve boots the persistent KEK-sealed workload-identity root, single-cut replacing the ephemeral root, and surfaces typed cause-distinct refusals on a bad KEK / envelope. Closes the D-CA-4 deferral and folds GH #215 (boot-side). Tier-3 Lima; captures EDD D01 (root key never plaintext at rest) + O04 (refuse-to-start, cause-distinct, no re-mint). Implements D-OC-4/5/6. Does NOT depend on Phase 01.

steps.name: Typed CA-boot failure stays cause-distinguishable at the operator boundary
steps.description: Typed CA-boot failures remain cause-distinguishable when they reach the control-plane error boundary: a boot failure surfaces its underlying cause (wrong-KEK vs tampered-envelope vs absent-KEK) without being flattened to a string. This is the additive error surface 02-02 depends on; no underlying CA-error change is needed (the cause variants are already distinct).
criteria: A CA-boot failure converts into the control-plane error type through ? with no string flattening — it stays matchable as a distinct CA-boot failure at the composition root.

Each boot cause (absent-KEK, envelope-decrypt over wrong-KEK, envelope-decrypt over tampered-envelope) is matchable as the CA-boot failure and renders a cause string that preserves the distinct underlying cause (wrong-KEK and tampered render different strings).
The CA-boot failure maps to a 500 at the HTTP boundary for exhaustiveness only (the boot path never reaches HTTP).
The error surface is additive — no existing control-plane error variant changes shape; the crate compiles clean.
review_checklist: Dedicated #[from] variant mirroring ViewStoreBoot / Tls / Cgroup — NEVER a ControlPlaneError::internal flatten (development.md § Errors).
No underlying CaError change in scope: wrong-KEK vs tampered are already distinct Display variants and the boot error embeds the typed cause as #[source].
steps.implementation_notes: Acceptance, example-based — a finite enumerated set, so a parametrized table over the three causes (Mandate M5/M11). Assert on matchability + rendered cause-string distinctness, not private fields. The in-process typed-error invariant O04 rides on (O04 capture is 02-03). Default lane, no real I/O.

steps.name: Wire the persistent CA boot into run_server
steps.description: overdrive serve's composition root boots the persistent KEK-sealed root, single-cut replacing the ephemeral workload-identity root: the adopted persistent root (not a fresh per-boot root) backs IdentityMgr, the Earned-Trust probes (KEK-resolve, envelope-decrypt) run at production boot, and a boot failure propagates as the typed CA-boot failure from 02-01. The exact boot call shape is pinned in DESIGN (feature-delta C1); reuse it as-is, only newly called from production.
criteria: The ephemeral workload-identity root composition is deleted (single-cut); run_server boots the persistent root, threading the real redb store path so refuse-to-start remediation names the actual file.
The workload-identity CA backing IdentityMgr is the adopted persistent root, not a fresh in-memory root per boot.
A boot failure propagates through ? as the typed CA-boot failure (no flatten) and surfaces its cause-distinct message.
The separate operator/control-plane HTTPS CA is unchanged — the two CAs are not conflated.
Compile-check + integration --no-run GREEN under Lima (runtime RED->GREEN of the serve scaffolds is confirmed in 02-03).
review_checklist: git diff confirms only the workload-identity CA composition changed; the operator HTTPS CA (mint_ephemeral_ca) is untouched (CLAUDE.md two-CA discipline).
ca_boot reused as-is (Reuse Analysis: REUSE AS-IS, only newly called) — no signature/logic change; if the DESIGN boot signature diverges from C1, STOP and surface the gap.
Single-cut: the ephemeral block deletes in the same commit (no flag, no parallel path).
steps.implementation_notes: Integration / wiring per testing.md — the contract is 'run_server composes the persistent CA correctly and the probes run'; the driving port is the overdrive serve subprocess (EDD surface captured in 02-03). This step's gate is the compile-check + the wiring landing.

steps.name: GREEN serve boot integration scaffolds + capture EDD D01 + O04
steps.description: GREEN the serve-boot Lima scaffolds slice by slice — first-boot persist (S-OC-06), adopt-on-restart no-remint (S-OC-07), refuse on wrong-KEK / tampered-envelope / absent-KEK (S-OC-08a/b/c), pairwise-distinct refusal stderr (S-OC-08d), and refuse-to-start leaves the root unchanged with no silent re-mint (S-OC-09) — plus the rotate-correlation IssueSvid executor-dispatch scaffold S-OC-10 (direct action-shim dispatch coverage; no Phase 01 dependency). Then capture EDD D01 and O04 against the built overdrive binary, SHA-pinned in Lima.
criteria: S-OC-06 (EDD D01): serve first boot generates a P-256 root + node intermediate, persists the root as a KEK-sealed AEAD envelope in the IntentStore file, and reaches serving; the on-disk file contains the envelope fields and NO plaintext root-key DER (byte-scan).

S-OC-07 (EDD D01): serve restart with the same KEK decrypts and adopts the SAME root (identical serial across restart), generates no new root, and the on-disk file still contains no plaintext key bytes.

S-OC-08a/b/c (EDD O04): serve refuses to start on wrong-KEK, tampered-envelope, and absent-KEK (the latter before any issuance, no throwaway KEK); each refusal names its cause + the IntentStore path in stderr, emits health.startup.refused, and the control plane does NOT begin serving.
S-OC-08d (EDD O04): the wrong-KEK, tampered-envelope, and absent-KEK stderr cause strings are pairwise distinct.

S-OC-09 (EDD O04): after a refused boot, re-supplying the correct KEK adopts the SAME original root (identical serial) and no new root envelope was written during the refused boot (no silent re-mint).

S-OC-10: a rotate-correlation IssueSvid dispatches through the existing action-shim executor — a fresh issued_certificates row (new serial/window) is observable, IdentityMgr hold-replaces the freshly-minted material, and the held serial matches the new audit-row serial (no new executor surface).

EDD satisfied gate: D01 and O04 each carry Lima-captured SHA-pinned evidence (D01 shows the byte-scan finds no plaintext key; O04 shows the 3 cause-distinct stderr + the no-remint serial check); each is set to satisfied ONLY after a different-fox Haiku reviewer per expectation refutes-or-confirms the evidence (the authoring agent never self-stamps).
review_checklist: S-OC-10 is direct executor-dispatch coverage (a rotate-correlation IssueSvid constructed in the test and dispatched through the existing executor) — no Phase 01 reconcile-branch flip needed, no Phase 01 dependency.
The action-shim executor (issue_svid) is reused unchanged — assert the audit row + held snapshot, not executor internals.
EDD runners stay black-box (no overdrive-* link); captures run via run-expectation.sh in Lima.
steps.implementation_notes: Integration / Tier-3 Lima per testing.md — #[ignore]-gated subprocess tests over the built serve binary. Verify side effects: on-disk redb byte-scan (D01), stderr cause + refuse outcome (O04), no-remint serial comparison. Remove #[ignore] one scaffold at a time, confirm fail-for-right-reason first. Scaffolds exist (b8d0546f) — do not re-author. D01/O04 satisfied only after a different-fox Haiku reviewer reads evidence/ adversarially (verification.md).

phases.name: Consumer-side #215 — issued-cert summary surfaced + E03 chain proof
phases.description: Surface the current workload-SVID summary to the operator via an additive issued-certificates field on the alloc-status response (no cert bytes / no key), render it in overdrive alloc status, and complete the E03 full-chain-verifies proof to all three sub-claims. Tier-3 Lima; captures EDD O05 (operator-legible audit row) + E03 (full chain verifies). Implements D-OC-7 and the review-design Medium-1 E03 3-sub-claim obligation. Depends on Phase 02 (a booted persistent CA) for the O05 fixture; does NOT depend on Phase 01.

steps.name: Additive issued-certificates summary on the alloc-status response + server aggregation
steps.description: The alloc-status response gains an additive issued-certificates summary (serial, spiffe_id, issuer_serial, not_after — NO cert bytes, NO key), and the alloc-status handler projects, per running alloc, the max-issuance_ordinal audit row matching that alloc's SPIFFE identity. Reuse the existing audit-row type, observation query, and SPIFFE-for-allocation helper as-is — no row-schema or rkyv envelope change. The exact wire shape is pinned in DESIGN (feature-delta D1).
criteria: The alloc-status response exposes an issued-certificates summary carrying exactly serial, spiffe_id, issuer_serial, not_after — no cert PEM/DER, no private key.
The server projects, per running alloc, the single max-issuance_ordinal issued-certificate row whose SPIFFE identity matches that alloc — NOT history, NOT non-matching rows.
The field is JSON backward-compatible: an empty issued-certificates summary is omitted from the serialized response, so existing consumers see no change.
No row-schema / rkyv envelope change to the audit row; the observation query and the SPIFFE-for-allocation helper are reused unchanged.
Compile-check GREEN under Lima.
review_checklist: Audit-row type, issued_certificate_rows() query, and SpiffeId::for_allocation reused as-is (Reuse Analysis: REUSE AS-IS) — no new field/method, no rkyv envelope bump.
Newtype discipline: serial is CertSerial, spiffe_id is SpiffeId — no raw String for domain ids.
Persist-inputs spirit: project from the audit-row facts; cache no derived 'current cert'.
steps.implementation_notes: Integration / wiring per testing.md — the AC is a wiring + projection contract; runtime assertions live in the 03-02 Lima scaffolds, this step's gate is the additive field + projection compiling. Assert observable response shape, never private handler internals. O05 capture is 03-03.

steps.name: `overdrive alloc status` CLI render of the issued-certificates section
steps.description: overdrive alloc status renders each issued-certificate summary (serial / spiffe_id / issuer_serial / not_after) with NO cert bytes and NO key, then GREENs the Lima scaffolds S-OC-11 (the surfaced serial matches the minted cert) and S-OC-12 (the render shows exactly the max-issuance_ordinal row per running alloc, not history, and leaks no cert bytes/key).
criteria: S-OC-11 (EDD O05): overdrive alloc status --job <id> for a deployed running workload surfaces an issued-certificate section showing serial, spiffe_id, issuer_serial, not_after, and the surfaced serial matches the minted certificate's serial.

S-OC-12 (EDD O05): for a running alloc with multiple issued-certificate rows over time (first issue + a re-mint), the render shows exactly the max-issuance_ordinal row per running alloc (NOT history), contains NO certificate PEM/DER and NO private key, and a post-restart serial change reads as the current cert rather than an anomaly.
The CLI render is purely additive — alloc status output for a workload with no issued certs is unchanged (empty section omitted).
Compile-check GREEN and the two Lima scaffolds confirmed fail-for-right-reason before GREEN.
steps.placement_correction: 2026-06-10 (orchestrator, user-approved): the DISTILL scaffold was authored in crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/built_in_ca_operator_composition/alloc_status_issued_certificates.rs, but S-OC-11/12 are CLI-render driving-port tests and overdrive-control-plane CANNOT depend on overdrive-cli (cycle) — so the test is structurally unrunnable there. Relocated single-cut into the existing overdrive-cli CLI alloc-status home (crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs), driven in-process via overdrive_cli::render::alloc_status_* per overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md (no-subprocess) and the 02-03 serve_persistent_ca.rs in-process-run_server precedent (same commit b8d0546f). The control-plane scaffold + its mod line in tests/integration.rs are deleted in the same commit (no stub).
review_checklist: If the render lives in a different overdrive-cli path than commands/alloc.rs (e.g. a shared render module), use the actual file but stay within overdrive-cli render + alloc command; if the seam is ambiguous, STOP and surface it.
Render via the summary fields' Display — no raw String plumbing of domain ids.
steps.implementation_notes: Integration per testing.md — the driving port is overdrive_cli::render::alloc_status_* called IN-PROCESS over a constructed AllocStatusResponse (the established overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs pattern; overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md forbids subprocess-spawning the binary). The scaffold header's 'real subprocess in Lima' wording is boilerplate the sibling 02-03 serve_persistent_ca.rs already resolved against ('NOT a forked overdrive binary'). Verify the rendered section + its fields (serial/spiffe_id/issuer_serial/not_after via Display) and the ABSENCE of cert-bytes/key tokens (S-OC-12 no-leak invariant, ADR-0067 #215-boundary); section omitted when issued_certificates is empty (additive). Author S-OC-11/12 in alloc_status.rs as s_03_xx-style render tests; delete the misplaced control-plane scaffold + its tests/integration.rs mod line single-cut. Run under Lima (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run, no --no-run). O05 capture is 03-03.

steps.name: Extend E03 runner to 3 sub-claims + capture EDD O05 + E03
steps.description: Capture EDD O05 (issued-certificates audit row operator-visible) against the GREEN CLI surface, and complete EDD E03 (full chain verifies) by extending the E03 runner from its current 2-check shape to all three sub-claims: chain verifies, leaf profile, and the pathLen=0 negative anchor (a further-CA chain MUST fail verification). The existing chain-verify test gains an env-gated PEM export (fixture change; the existing tests stay GREEN) feeding the runner; no new production API and no chain-export operator verb (D-CA-4 holds — openssl verify is the honest external entry point).
criteria: O05 satisfied: the captured alloc status evidence shows the issued-certificate summary (serial/spiffe_id/issuer_serial/not_after) is operator-legible and carries no cert bytes/key (graduates S-OC-11/12); satisfied only after a different-fox Haiku reviewer confirms the evidence is executed (not narrated) against the built binary in Lima.
S-OC-13 (E03 sub-claim 1): openssl verify of the exported svid against the exported root + untrusted intermediate exits 0 (svid: OK).
S-OC-14 (E03 sub-claim 2): the exported leaf carries exactly one spiffe:// URI SAN, basicConstraints CA:FALSE, and keyUsage digitalSignature marked critical.
S-OC-15 (E03 sub-claim 3, MANDATORY negative anchor): a chain where the pathLen=0 intermediate signs a further-CA cert FAILS openssl verify (non-zero exit) — pathLen is ENFORCED, not merely set.

The E03 runner enforces all three sub-claims before any satisfied (extended from 2 to 3 checks); the existing chain-verify tests stay GREEN (the PEM export is an env-gated fixture addition, not a behavioural change); the runner stays black-box (bash + openssl + file-observation, no overdrive-* link).

E03 satisfied gate: satisfied ONLY after a different-fox Haiku reviewer per expectation reads the captured evidence/ adversarially and confirms all three sub-claims executed; E03 evidence missing the pathLen=0 negative anchor (sub-claim 3) is a MANDATORY refuted, even if sub-claims 1-2 executed cleanly.
review_checklist: The export hook on the chain-verify test is an env-gated fixture write (PEMs written before the TempDir drops); the existing tests stay GREEN.
PEMs come from the existing Ca port surface — NO new production API, NO operator verb that mints/exports a chain (D-CA-4 holds).
Runner extended 2 -> 3 checks; cargo nextest invoked only as the PEM producer, runner otherwise black-box.
Dep on 02-02 is for a booted persistent CA backing the O05 fixture.
steps.implementation_notes: Integration / Tier-3 Lima per testing.md (S-OC-15 is an enumerated sad path). O05/E03 are operator-surface black-box evidence; the in-process invariants are covered by the Tier-3 alloc-status tests (O05) and the existing GREEN chain-verify tests (E03). E03 runner extension to 3 checks is MANDATORY (review-design Medium-1): a 2-check runner green on sub-claims 1-2 alone does NOT satisfy E03. Capture via run-expectation.sh, SHA-pinned; satisfied only after a different-fox Haiku reviewer who MUST refute E03 evidence missing sub-claim 3.
